name: Markdown to Card MCP Server
what_it_does: Markdown to Card MCP server enabling AI agents to convert Markdown text into visually styled card images — transforming formatted text, code snippets, and structured content into shareable card images for social media, documentation, and content publishing workflows, enabling agents to produce visual content from text without manual design work.
use_cases: Converting blog post highlights to shareable social media cards from content agents
Generating visual code snippet cards for developer content from marketing agents
Creating visual quote cards from article content from social media agents
Producing styled documentation cards for sharing from documentation agents
Automating visual content creation from text-heavy outputs from productivity agents
Building content marketing pipelines with visual card generation from automation agents
not_for: Full graphic design work (Markdown cards are simple styled text, not rich graphics)
High-volume image generation (use dedicated image generation APIs for scale)
best_when: An agent needs to quickly produce visually attractive cards from Markdown text — for social media sharing, documentation snippets, or visual content creation from text output.
avoid_when: You need complex graphic design, animations, or image editing beyond styled text rendering.

auth.notes: No authentication — local rendering tool. No external service required.

pricing.notes: Free, open source community MCP for visual card generation.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no external rate limits; rendering speed depends on hardware

known_agent_gotchas: Card styling options may be limited — custom themes require MCP configuration or code changes
Font rendering quality depends on system fonts installed
Output image format and resolution may be fixed — verify if your target platform requires specific specs
Community MCP with minimal documentation — discover available templates by exploration
Headless rendering may require additional dependencies (e.g., chromium, puppeteer, or canvas library)
Generated card content is text-based — rich media (charts, diagrams) not typically supported
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local rendering. No external calls. No credentials. Card content stays local. Community MCP. Safe for general use.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local rendering. No external calls. No credentials. Safe community MCP.
